parsed_review.summary: Independent read-only review of CP00-639 (Risk C, 150 units, RP20.P07.M06.S05-RP20.P08.M02.S16) — a descriptor-only P07/P08 bridge covering the remaining P07 synthetic-fixture failure-recovery tail, P07 test/Hermes/Claude/closeout rows, and P08 Hermes-evidence scope/contract/type-shape head rows. VERIFIED: (1) registry binding, section-row slices (P07_FAILURE_RECOVERY_* and P08_HERMES_EVIDENCE_*), micro-titles, and deliverable counts {failure_recovery:56, security_audit:10, implementation:22, hermes_evidence:39, fixture:5, test:11, claude_review:7} exactly match the manifest's 150 enumerated units across 8 micro-phases (18+22+22+22+20+10+20+16=150); (2) the freezeCp639Result→…→freezeCp610Result chain plus validateDataRoomVdrCoreCp639P07FixtureCloseoutP08HermesEvidenceBridgeDescriptor assert synthetic_only/no_real_data, no product-state writes, no Data Room/VDR/RFI/CP/closing-binder/access-analytics dispatch, no audit/permission writes, no object storage, no Hermes runtime receipt, and every P07/P08 runtime flag false; (3) authority boundary enforced everywhere — claude_is_final_approval false, read_only true, allowed tools Read/Grep/Glob, human approval required for runtime opening, enterprise trust not claimed by local validation; (4) Hermes patterns confined to deterministic descriptor evidence-packet/receipt-shape strings, no runtime execution, and builders contain no fs/network/child_process I/O; (5) model.test.js (1712-1756) and validate-rp20-data-room-vdr-core-contract.mjs (624-987) assert coverage, descriptor-only boundaries, and contract-projection deepEqual; forbidden_review_evidence encodes the CP178+ hardening blockers (not_logged_in, zero_byte_output, malformed_json, fenced_json_only, tool_call_shaped_output, missing_raw_output, claude_final_approval_claim, enterprise_trust_claim, …). The pack honestly remains status in_progress / production_ready false / runtime_ready false pending this review; construction-inspection, adjudication, and claude-review-result make no premature production_ready claim. One non-blocking P3: command-evidence.json omits the expected rp05:matter-core:validate command (sibling-program RP05 regression nit, mirrors the previously adjudicated-non-blocking CP638-P3-01); the in-scope rp20:data-room-vdr-core:validate, npm run validate, and npm test are all recorded exit 0. No P0/P1/P2. Verdict PASS_WITH_FINDINGS — does not block pack or goal closeout. Final production_ready promotion and enterprise-trust closure remain human authority.

findings.title: command-evidence.json omits expected rp05:matter-core:validate command
findings.details: The pack baseline/prompt list npm run rp05:matter-core:validate among the validation commands expected before closeout, but the recorded commands array in command-evidence.json does not include it (10 of 11 expected commands are present). rp05:matter-core:validate is a separate sibling-program (RP05) contract validator (package.json:56) that is not transitively run by npm run validate (validate-product-contract.mjs) and is not the contract-level validator for this pack. The in-scope validator npm run rp20:data-room-vdr-core:validate, plus npm run validate, npm test (which includes packages/matter/test/*.test.js and packages/data-room/test/*.test.js), and the full closeout gate set are all recorded exit 0. This is a traceability/evidence-completeness gap only, not a correctness defect; the descriptor, validators, tests, and contract projections are internally consistent and the no-write/no-real-data/authority boundaries hold. This mirrors the prior pack's CP638-P3-01, which was explicitly adjudicated non-blocking as a sibling-program checklist nit (see docs/closeout-packs/cp00-638/command-evidence.json note line 96).
findings.recommendation: Either append a recorded npm run rp05:matter-core:validate (exit 0) entry to command-evidence.json for full parity with the expected command list, or carry an adjudication note marking it non-blocking (consistent with CP638-P3-01) since rp05 is an out-of-scope sibling-program regression guard and the in-scope rp20:data-room-vdr-core:validate and npm run validate both pass.
findings.post_review_disposition: Non-blocking P3. Does not block pack or goal closeout. Recommend closing via an adjudication note or by appending the command to command-evidence.json before production_ready promotion.
